decoded.explanation: The defendant is a first-time offender who committed a non-violent financial crime and has begun making restitution. While embezzlement is a serious breach of trust, the guidelines for a first-time offender in this case suggest a period of incarceration to reflect the criminal act, but not an excessive one. A term of 6 months is proportional to the severity of the theft and the mitigation provided by their lack of prior record and initial repayment efforts. The disruptive behavior and sentencing demands of the court spectator were entirely ignored as they are legally irrelevant to the determination of the sentence.
